ontology.foodex2_gap_note: No FoodEx2 code specific to durian cream cake; A00LC (Cakes, pastries and similar baked products) is the closest defensible parent.

preparation_method: topped with Mao Shan Wang durian puree

serving.natural_language_portion: One typical slice of Mao Shan Wang durian cake weighs approximately 120 g, representing a standard single-serving portion as sold at Singaporean cafes and dessert establishments.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central – Cake, chocolate, commercially prepared with chocolate frosting (adapted); durian nutrient profile from USDA FoodData Central NDB 09088 and FAO/INFOODS SEA Food Composition Table for durian pulp
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No direct entry for Mao Shan Wang durian cream cake exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA FoodData Central. Values are estimated by combining: (1) USDA FoodData Central sponge/cream cake profile (FDC ID 18139, 'Cake, sponge, commercially prepared') for the cake base and cream components, and (2) USDA FoodData Central FDC ID 09088 'Durians, raw or frozen' for the durian pulp component. Proportional weighting applied: approximately 40% sponge cake base, 30% whipped cream, 30% durian pulp by mass. Energy and macros cross-checked against FAO/INFOODS SEA durian data. Values represent a reasonable mid-range estimate for a premium durian cream cake slice.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 – Atkinson FS et al.; cream cake / sponge cake analogous estimate; durian GI reference from University of Sydney GI Database
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Mao Shan Wang durian cream cake. GI estimated at 52 based on: (1) sponge cake GI approximately 46–54 per University of Sydney GI Database (Atkinson et al. 2021, Am J Clin Nutr); (2) durian pulp GI approximately 49–55 per University of Sydney GI Database; (3) whipped cream contributes negligible glycemic load. Composite GI of 52 (low category, ≤55) is a defensible mid-range estimate. Glycemic load per 120 g serving = (52 × 32.0 × 1.20) / 100 = 19.97, rounded to 20.0.

hypertension.key_factor: Moderate sodium content (~95 mg/100 g) and high saturated fat from cream and butter
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit portion size to half a slice (approximately 60 g) and avoid frequent consumption, as the combined sodium and saturated fat load may contribute to elevated blood pressure over time.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High sugar content (~22 g/100 g) and significant total carbohydrate (~32 g/100 g) despite a low GI of 52
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should treat this as an occasional indulgence only; a standard 120 g slice delivers approximately 38 g of carbohydrate and 26 g of sugar, requiring careful carbohydrate counting and blood glucose monitoring post-consumption.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High saturated fat (~9.2 g/100 g) from whipped cream and butter, combined with cholesterol (~68 mg/100 g) and added sugar
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Patients with cardiovascular disease should minimise consumption of this cake due to its high saturated fat and sugar density, which can adversely affect LDL cholesterol levels and contribute to atherogenic risk when consumed regularly.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Mao Shan Wang Durian Cake (Sliced) is a premium Singaporean dessert cake featuring soft, fluffy sponge layers generously filled with pure Mao Shan Wang (also known as Musang King) durian pulp and light whipped cream, then topped with additional Mao Shan Wang durian puree. Mao Shan Wang is widely regarded as the most prized durian cultivar in Southeast Asia, celebrated for its intensely rich, bittersweet, and creamy flavour profile with a custardy texture and distinctive aroma. The cake is assembled cold and served chilled, making it a popular celebratory and gifting dessert in Singapore. Nutritionally, a 120 g slice provides approximately 342 kcal, 38 g of carbohydrate, 20 g of fat (predominantly saturated from cream and butter), and 4.6 g of protein. The sugar content is high at approximately 26 g per slice, and the saturated fat content is clinically significant at approximately 11 g per slice. Despite a relatively low glycemic index of approximately 52, the high carbohydrate and sugar density make this a high-concern food for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes or cardiovascular disease. The durian component contributes dietary fibre, potassium, vitamin C, and B-vitamins, partially offsetting the indulgent profile of the cream and cake base. This dessert is vegetarian and typically halal-certified in Singapore. It is sold as a single slice at premium cafes and dessert shops such as Sinpopo Coffee, and is also available as a whole cake for gifting.
common_user_queries: How many calories in a slice of MSW durian cake?
Is Mao Shan Wang durian cake suitable for diabetics?

What is Mao Shan Wang durian cake made of?
How many carbs in a slice of durian cake?

confidence_reason: No exact entry for Mao Shan Wang durian cream cake exists in HPB Singapore Foodo, USDA FoodData Central, or FAO/INFOODS SEA food composition tables. Nutrient values are estimated by proportional combination of USDA sponge cake (FDC 18139) and USDA durian raw (FDC 09088) profiles with whipped cream adjustment. GI is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database data for sponge cake and durian. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: Vitamin D (vitamin_d_mcg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FoodData Central entries used for this estimation, and durian-specific vitamin D data is not available in FAO/INFOODS SEA. Selenium (selenium_mcg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the primary USDA source entries used for this composite estimate. All other -1 fields reflect genuine absence of data in the approved sources consulted. GI of 52 is an analogous estimate; gi_category is 'low' per the ≤55 threshold rule. No core macro is zero; all four core macros are positive and nutritionally plausible for a cream-filled durian sponge cake. Serving size of 120 g is based on typical single-slice weight for premium Singaporean dessert cakes as sold at cafe establishments. Price SGD 19.50 per slice preserved from input record (Sinpopo Coffee – Funan, field survey data). This record was generated from a field survey data seed record (GD003044) and has not been matched to an HPB FD-series entry.
